title: Arthratherum rigidum
taxonomy: Arthratherum rigidum, also known as rigid oat grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Australia and New Zealand, where it is considered a common invasive species. Arthratherum rigidum is a perennial grass that can grow up to 1 meter tall and has a robust root system. This species is often found in coastal areas and is known to form dense stands that outcompete native vegetation. As a member of the Poaceae family, it is closely related to other grasses such as oats and wheat.

intro: Arthratherum rigidum, also known as Hard Needle Grass or Hard Needle, is a species of grass native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
